name: CLUE — Center for Learning and Undergraduate Enrichment
description: Free late-night drop-in tutoring for many lower-division courses including writing, math, chemistry, physics, statistics, and language. Open Sunday through Thursday evenings during the academic quarter at Mary Gates Hall.

name: Math Study Center
description: Drop-in help for students enrolled in lower-division UW math courses, including precalculus, calculus, and linear algebra. Staffed by graduate TAs and undergraduate tutors in Communications Building.

name: Odegaard Writing & Research Center (OWRC)
description: Free one-on-one consultations to work through any stage of a writing project — brainstorming, drafting, revision, or research. Open to all undergraduates regardless of major.

name: Instructional Center (IC)
description: OMA&D's academic support hub: tutoring across STEM and writing, study groups, study skills workshops, and computer access. Serves all UW students with priority for OMA&D-affiliated students.

name: Undergraduate Academic Advising
description: General advising for undeclared students and those exploring majors. Help with degree planning, navigating academic difficulty, and connecting to campus resources. Drop-in and scheduled appointments available.

name: Disability Resources for Students (DRS)
description: Confidential accommodations for students with disabilities, chronic health conditions, learning differences, mental health diagnoses, or temporary injuries. Coordinates testing accommodations, note-taking, and accessible coursework.

name: UW Counseling Center
description: Free short-term individual and group counseling for currently enrolled students. Same-day urgent appointments available during business hours. Located in Schmitz Hall.

name: Hall Health Mental Health Clinic
description: Psychiatric evaluation, medication management, and short-term psychotherapy for enrolled students. Most services billable through Husky Health insurance or out-of-pocket on a sliding scale.

name: LiveWell
description: Health promotion and advocacy for students affected by sexual assault, relationship violence, stalking, or substance-use concerns. Confidential advocacy and peer health education.

name: SafeCampus
description: Anonymous violence-prevention hotline for any UW community member worried about themselves or someone else. Trained counselors help assess risk and connect people to resources. Available 24/7 in many cases via the published line.

name: Husky HelpLine (24/7 mental health support)
description: Free 24/7 phone access to mental health support for enrolled UW students at all three campuses. Counselors can provide in-the-moment support and connect students to longer-term care.

name: Q Center
description: Community space and support for LGBTQIA+ students at UW. Drop-in lounge, identity-based discussion groups, advocacy, and connections to affirming health and counseling resources.

name: Any Hungry Husky Food Pantry
description: Free, no-questions-asked food pantry in Poplar Hall offering shelf-stable food, fresh produce when available, and hygiene supplies. Open to all UW students experiencing food insecurity.

name: LiveWell Basic Needs Navigation
description: LiveWell offers basic-needs navigation: confidential help connecting students to food, housing, SNAP/CalFresh enrollment, emergency funds, and other support systems. Talk to a peer wellness coach when you don't know where to start.

name: UW Emergency Aid
description: Short-term emergency funding for enrolled students across Seattle, Bothell, and Tacoma campuses facing unexpected hardships — medical costs, housing emergencies, food, transportation, or other urgent needs that could disrupt enrollment. Aid may come as grants, short-term loans, or community-resource connections.

name: Emergency Housing Resources (UW Libraries Guide)
description: Curated guide aggregating UW and community emergency-housing resources for students experiencing or at risk of housing insecurity. Includes short-term shelter contacts, eviction-prevention programs, and case-management referrals.

name: U-PASS (Husky Card transit pass)
description: Unlimited rides on regional transit — Sound Transit, King County Metro, Community Transit, Pierce Transit, Kitsap Transit, and the Seattle Streetcar — included in service & activities fees for most students.

name: UW Transportation Services
description: Parking permits, bike commuting resources, carpool & vanpool matching, late-night NightRide shuttle, and general commute planning for students, staff, and faculty.

name: Commuter & Transfer Commons
description: Lounge, lockers, microwaves, and community programming for commuter and transfer students. A homebase between classes for students who don't live on campus.

name: NightRide Shuttle
description: Free late-night door-to-door shuttle service for UW students, staff, and faculty within a defined zone around campus. Designed to help people get home safely after dark.

name: Odegaard Undergraduate Library
description: 24/5 undergraduate library with quiet floors, collaborative spaces, the OWRC writing center, IT help, a computer commons, and the Husky Grind cafe. Central undergraduate study hub.

name: Suzzallo & Allen Libraries
description: The main UW research libraries. Suzzallo's Reading Room is a landmark silent study space; Allen Library houses subject-specific collections, reservable group study rooms, and special collections.

name: Husky Union Building (HUB) Study Spaces
description: Lounges, booths, reservable meeting rooms, dining, and event spaces in the student union. Great for group projects, casual study, or meeting clubs and student organizations.

name: UW Branch Libraries
description: Smaller subject-focused libraries — Engineering, Health Sciences, Foster Business, Built Environments, Music, and more — often quieter than the main libraries and tailored to specific disciplines.

name: Career & Internship Center
description: One-on-one career coaching, resume and cover-letter reviews, mock interviews, career fairs, and the Handshake job board. Open to all undergraduates regardless of major.

name: Handshake (UW Career Platform)
description: Official UW job and internship platform connecting students with employers actively recruiting Huskies. Apply to part-time jobs, internships, and full-time roles; RSVP to events and career fairs.

name: UW Student Employment
description: On-campus job listings including work-study and non-work-study positions across libraries, dining, athletics, research labs, and administrative offices. Often a flexible fit for class schedules.

name: Undergraduate Research Program (URP)
description: Connects undergraduates with faculty-mentored research across every discipline. Offers funding, a research database, info sessions, and the annual Undergraduate Research Symposium.

name: Office of Student Financial Aid
description: Manages FAFSA, WASFA, grants, loans, work-study, and need-based scholarships for UW students. First stop for questions about your financial-aid package or unexpected changes in your financial situation.

name: Student Fiscal Services
description: Handles tuition billing, payment plans, refunds, and 1098-T tax forms. Talk to them about payment-plan options, late fees, or holds on your account that block registration.

name: UW Scholarship Search
description: Searchable database of UW-administered scholarships filterable by major, identity, year, and need. Many awards require only the FAFSA/WASFA plus a short essay.
